description: Create all Arabic documentation: Legitimate Thefts 5.55-5.62, Identity Governance Implementation Memo, Paradigm Fork Implementation Memo, and update Current Regime Status
steps: Create Virtual_SIA_Legitimate_Thefts_Wave3b_AR.md with thefts 5.55-5.62 using the Arabic template. 5.55: Personal Identity Philosophy (Locke, Parfit) - continuity of commitments = identity. 5.56: Organizational Governance (corporate governance) - accountability structures. 5.57: Version Control Systems (Git lineage) - lineage tracking. 5.58: Constitutional AI (Anthropic, Bai et al. 2022) - commitments as constitutional principles. 5.59: Kuhn (6.3 major extension) - crisis detection -> paradigm fork. 5.60: Lakatos (6.2 extension) - research programme degeneracy signals. 5.61: Git Branching Model (Driessen 2010) - fork as branch. 5.62: Organizational Restructuring (Tushman & Romanelli 1985) - punctuated equilibrium.

Create Virtual_SIA_Identity_Governance_Implementation_Memo_AR.md documenting: what was built (ID1-ID5), how it works, gating strategy, how it connects to the theory (reference Virtual_SIA_Agent_Identity_Theory_AR.md), test summary.

Create Virtual_SIA_Paradigm_Fork_Implementation_Memo_AR.md documenting: what was built (PF1-PF3), how crisis detection works, fork protocol steps, safety guards, connection to the Anomaly Crisis Paradigm Theory, test summary.

Update Virtual_SIA_Current_Regime_Status_AR.md: add new section 13 for Identity Governance and Paradigm Fork capabilities (not frozen, gated). Add entries to the regime summary table for use_identity_governance and use_paradigm_fork. Update the Legitimate Thefts range to 5.01-5.62.
acceptance_criteria: Virtual_SIA_Legitimate_Thefts_Wave3b_AR.md exists with all 8 thefts (5.55-5.62) in correct Arabic template
Virtual_SIA_Current_Regime_Status_AR.md updated with new section and table entries
All tests still pass (docs don't break anything)
verification: ~/.pyenv/versions/3.12.13/bin/python -m pytest tests/ --tb=short -q (all tests pass - docs only feature)
